title: cuphea rionegrensis
intro: Cuphea rionegrensis, also known as the Pink Waxflower, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The Pink Waxflower is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, as well as supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine and holistic health practices.
